generated_oml:
// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance InputCurrentSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Motor current sensor integrated with the MABI MAX 100 first vertical axis motor-gearbox assembly, measuring real-time electrical input current for friction analysis"
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorCurrentDataTransmitted
]

instance PositionEncoderLeft : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Primary position encoder on the left side of the MABI MAX 100 first vertical axis drive, measuring output angle with high precision for motion accuracy validation"
    DTDFVocab:producedData PositionEncoderLeftDataTransmitted
]

instance PositionEncoderRight : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Secondary position encoder on the right side of the MABI MAX 100 first vertical axis drive, providing redundant angle measurement for data validation and cross-checking"
    DTDFVocab:producedData PositionEncoderRightDataTransmitted
]

instance TemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Temperature sensor monitoring operational temperature of the cycloidal gear mechanism in the MABI MAX 100 first vertical axis, used as secondary parameter for friction modeling"
    DTDFVocab:producedData TemperatureDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance MotorCurrentDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom InputCurrentSensor
]

instance PositionEncoderLeftDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom PositionEncoderLeft
]

instance PositionEncoderRightDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom PositionEncoderRight
]

instance TemperatureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom TemperatureSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance FrictionTraceData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated contextual friction traces derived from processed motor current, position encoder data, and temperature measurements for diagnostic analysis of gearbox friction"
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorCurrentDataTransmitted, PositionEncoderLeftDataTransmitted, PositionEncoderRightDataTransmitted, TemperatureDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLModelEnabler, SimulinkSimulationEnabler
]

instance MotionAccuracyModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Behavioral model representing the dynamic behavior of the cycloidal gear mechanism and motor assembly in the MABI MAX 100 first vertical axis, used for validating motion accuracy through simulation"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkSimulationEnabler
]

instance SysMLComponentModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Component & connector model of the MABI MAX 100 first vertical axis motor-gearbox assembly created using SysML Block Definition Diagrams (BDDs) and Internal Block Diagrams (IBDs)"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLModelEnabler
]

instance FrictionModelWhiteBox : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "White-box friction model implemented using state machines in SysML v1.7, representing detailed physical behavior of friction in the cycloidal gear mechanism"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLModelEnabler
]

instance FrictionModelBlackBox : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Black-box friction model processing aggregated sensor data to predict friction behavior without explicit physical parameterization"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataAggregationEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance SysMLModelEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SysML-based modeling environment enabling component & connector modeling of the motor-gearbox assembly through Block Definition Diagrams and Internal Block Diagrams"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataAggregationService, VisualizationService
]

instance SimulinkSimulationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MathWorks MATLAB Simulink environment providing executable simulation models for dynamic behavior analysis of the gearbox and motor system, including friction loss validation"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataAggregationService, MotionAccuracyValidationService
]

instance DataAggregationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Processing elements aggregating and abstracting real-time sensor data traces into contextual friction traces for analysis purposes"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataAggregationService
]

// C6: Services
instance DataAggregationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Passive, one-way data aggregation service collecting and processing real-time sensor data (torque, position, temperature) from the motor-gearbox assembly to derive contextual friction traces"
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionBehaviorInsight, WearPatternInsight
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Dashboard interface service displaying aggregated data for monitoring friction behavior, wear patterns, and operational anomalies without direct feedback to the physical system"
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionBehaviorInsight, WearPatternInsight, OperationalAnomalyInsight
]

instance MotionAccuracyValidationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Simulation-based service validating motion accuracy of the robot axis through behavioral modeling in Simulink"
    DTDFVocab:provides MotionAccuracyInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance FrictionBehaviorInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight into the dynamic friction behavior of the cycloidal gear mechanism derived from processed sensor data and friction models"
]

instance WearPatternInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Analysis of wear patterns in the gearbox components identified through contextual data trace aggregation and friction modeling"
]

instance OperationalAnomalyInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Detection of operational anomalies in the robot axis based on real-time monitoring of sensor data and model predictions"
]

instance MotionAccuracyInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Validation results for motion accuracy of the MABI MAX 100 first vertical axis derived from simulation-based behavioral modeling"
]

// C18: Environment
instance RobotAxisEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains InputCurrentSensor, PositionEncoderLeft, PositionEncoderRight, TemperatureSensor
]

// C19: Components
instance MotorGearboxAssembly : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Primary physical component of the MABI MAX 100 first vertical axis consisting of a cycloidal gear mechanism and integrated motor controlled by Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl controller"
    base:isContainedIn RobotAxisEnvironment
]

instance PositionEncoderAssembly : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Dual-encoder assembly providing redundant angle measurements for the MABI MAX 100 first vertical axis drive"
    base:isContainedIn RobotAxisEnvironment
]

instance TemperatureMonitoringSystem : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Temperature monitoring infrastructure integrated with the gearbox assembly for operational temperature measurement"
    base:isContainedIn RobotAxisEnvironment
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system’s temporal aspects are governed by the real-time synchronization requirements between physical and digital components, particularly in the context of digital shadows where data flows are automated and one-way. The text explicitly references the need for live data aggregation and abstraction, implying that temporal granularity must align with operational demands (e.g., sensor data streaming via MQTT). However, no explicit quantitative thresholds (e.g., synchronization frequencies, latency requirements) are provided. The modeling and simulation tools (e.g., MathWorks Simulink, other simulation methods) are integrated to handle behavioral descriptions, but their temporal governance (e.g., adaptive time scales) is not detailed beyond the implicit requirement for real-time processing of incoming data traces. No explicit mention of low-latency processing or adaptive synchronization mechanisms is made in the provided documents."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system’s orchestration and coordination are framed within the Digital Shadow Reference Model (Kritzinger et al.), which distinguishes Digital Twins from shadows by their ability to include active components for data interpretation and processing. The system leverages Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE) methodologies and tools (e.g., SysML, MontiArc, Simulink) to describe functional and logical architectures, including behavioral aspects via state machines or automata. While the text references the use of ADLs for component and connector modeling, it does not explicitly detail resource management frameworks or orchestration patterns. The integration of heterogeneous tools (e.g., simulation tools, general-purpose languages like C++/Java) suggests implicit coordination, but no specific architecture patterns (e.g., microservices, event-driven) or system-wide coordination mechanisms are described."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "
The lifecycle of a Digital Twin (DT) system is structured through **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** methodologies, encompassing multiple abstraction layers: **requirements, functional architecture, logical architecture, and product/technical layers**. These layers are managed via methodologies like **RFLP (Requirements, Functions, Logics, Physics)**, **OOSEM**, **SPES**, or **SMArDT**, which define phased transitions between abstraction levels. The lifecycle governance integrates heterogeneous stakeholder perspectives and involves **architecture description languages (ADLs)** such as **ACME, AADL, MontiArc, or SysML Internal Block Diagrams (IBDs)** for component-connector modeling, complemented by **state machines (SysML v1.7) or MontiArc Automaton** for behavioral representation. Transition management between phases is facilitated by **heterogeneous modeling tools** (e.g., **MathWorks Simulink, general-purpose simulation tools, or programming languages like C++/Java**), ensuring seamless integration of structural and behavioral models across lifecycle stages. The lifecycle is further governed by **interoperability frameworks** to align diverse stakeholder inputs and toolchain outputs, though explicit governance frameworks (e.g., ISO/IEC standards) are not detailed in the provided documents.
"
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin system addresses **fidelity and validity** through:
- **Model fidelity requirements**: Achieved via **multi-layered MBSE methodologies** (e.g., **RFLP [18]–[19], OOSEM [20], SPES [21], SMArDT [22]**) to ensure **consistent abstraction** across requirements, functions, logic, and physics layers,
- **Validation and verification (V&V) methods**: Implicitly supported by **ADLs (e.g., AADL [24])** for structural correctness and **simulation tools (e.g., Simulink [28])** for behavioral validation, though **specific V&V protocols** (e.g., formal methods, testing frameworks) are not detailed,
- **Uncertainty sources and quantification**: Not explicitly addressed in the document,
- **Error detection and correction mechanisms**: Not mentioned in the document,
- **Quality assurance processes**: Implicitly enforced via **heterogeneous stakeholder integration [31]** and **tool-specific workflows** (e.g., SysML for architecture, simulation tools for behavior), though **formal QA frameworks** (e.g., ISO standards, certification processes) are not referenced.
"
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The technical network infrastructure for this system leverages **MQTT** as the primary communication protocol for streaming time-series sensor data from physical objects to the digital representation. While the document does not specify network topology (e.g., star, mesh, or hierarchical), bandwidth requirements, or latency constraints, it implies a **low-latency, reliable data transmission** mechanism suitable for real-time or near-real-time applications. No redundancy mechanisms (e.g., failover, load balancing) or security protocols (e.g., TLS, authentication, encryption) for network communication are explicitly mentioned. The system’s reliance on passive data aggregation suggests minimal need for bidirectional control flows or explicit conflict resolution mechanisms."
]

    base:desc "The deployment infrastructure for this Digital Twin system is not explicitly detailed in the provided documents. The text references **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE) methodologies** and **architecture description languages (ADLs)** for modeling, but does not specify hosting platforms (cloud/edge/on-premise), computational/storage requirements, scalability mechanisms, or deployment models. The only related mention is the use of **simulation tools** (e.g., Simulink) and **general-purpose languages** (e.g., C++, Java) for behavioral modeling, which may imply on-premise or hybrid deployment but lacks concrete infrastructure details."

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system integrates with external systems through standardized data exchange protocols, including MQTT for streaming sensor data. The use of architecture description languages (ADLs) like SysML and MontiArc facilitates interoperability by defining interfaces, connectors, and compositional relationships. The system also relies on general-purpose programming languages (e.g., C++, Java) and simulation tools (e.g., Simulink) for behavioral modeling, enabling integration with enterprise systems and other Digital Twins. However, no explicit mention of interoperability standards (e.g., OPC UA, ROS) beyond MQTT is provided, and dependencies on external systems are implied rather than detailed."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin system adheres to **industry-specific modeling standards** but does not explicitly reference broader **ISO, IEC, or OMG standardization frameworks**. **SysML** is used as a modeling language without specifying compliance with the **OMG SysML standard** or version (e.g., SysML v1.7). **AADL** and **MontiArc** are employed for architecture description, but no mention is made of **SAE AS5506** (AADL standard) or **Model-Driven Architecture (MDA) framework** compliance. **Interoperability standards** are not explicitly discussed, though the integration of **heterogeneous tools (SysML, Simulink, general-purpose languages)** implies adherence to **de facto industry practices** for model exchange (e.g., XMI for SysML). **Certification requirements** or **compliance frameworks** (e.g., ISO 23247 for DTs) are not addressed in the documents. The absence of explicit references to **FMI (Functional Mock-up Interface)** or **ISO 15926** (for industrial data exchange) further underscores the lack of formal standardization details.
"
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin system described in the provided documents incorporates **MQTT (Message Queuing Telemetry Transport)** as a protocol for real-time data streaming between physical assets and their digital representations, as referenced in document [52]. While no explicit cybersecurity protocols (e.g., TLS, RBAC, or IEC 62443) are detailed, the **Asset Administration Shell (AAS)** framework (documents [46], [47], [50]) emphasizes **semantic traceability** of data lineage and **structured metadata management**, which indirectly supports data integrity and accountability. Additionally, the **physical safety cell** mentioned in document [44] for the MABI robotic arm (a Max-100 model) enforces spatial and operational constraints to mitigate physical hazards, though this pertains to **safety-critical system considerations** rather than cybersecurity. No explicit fail-safe mechanisms, risk assessment frameworks, or authentication systems (e.g., OPC UA security policies) are documented."
]
